title: Add MPK 8x384 10-minute submission record
body:
Add MPK 8x384 10-minute submission record

This PR adds a new 10-minute / 16 MB submission record:
- records/track_10min_16mb/2026-03-20_MPK_8x384

Highlights:
- model_family: mpk
- 8 layers, width 384
- 8 heads / 4 KV heads
- MPK strides k=2, m=4
- 80 FineWeb SP-1024 train shards
- wallclock stop at 599.527s
- final int8+zlib roundtrip val_bpb: 1.01558307
- total submission size int8+zlib: 14578273 bytes

Included files:
- README.md
- submission.json
- train.log
- train_gpt.py

comments.body: This PR currently includes the strongest completed run I have so far (\al_bpb: 1.01558307\). I’m attempting to gather additional seeds for statistical significance, but 8xH100 availability is currently constrained. I’ll add more run logs here as soon as I can.

﻿Added 2 supporting seed logs to the record folder:
- `seed=1338`: `final_int8_zlib_roundtrip_exact val_loss:2.35043404 val_bpb:1.04330173`
- `seed=1339`: `final_int8_zlib_roundtrip_exact val_loss:2.32680692 val_bpb:1.03281422`

Together with the original submitted run:
- `seed=1337`: `final_int8_zlib_roundtrip_exact val_loss:2.28798722 val_bpb:1.01558307`

3-seed mean:
- `val_bpb:1.03056634`

Important environment note: the two additional runs were gathered on a fresh 8x H100 instance that I provisioned without the exact earlier par-golf template environment. That host was materially slower than the original best run (`~238-280ms` step average vs `145.94ms` on the submitted run), so both supporting seeds stopped earlier under the same 600s wallclock cap (`2146` and `2480` steps vs `4108`).

I also had to use a small compatibility patch for hosts whose installed PyTorch lacked native `enable_gqa`; the rerun logs are included in the PR under the submission record folder.

﻿Added a clean matched-environment rerun log to the record folder:
- `seed=1340`: `final_int8_zlib_roundtrip_exact val_loss:2.28560860 val_bpb:1.01452726`

This run is the most directly comparable support seed so far:
- native `enable_gqa` environment
- `4094` steps before the 600s wallclock cap (vs `4108` on the original run)
- `step_avg:146.50ms` (vs `145.94ms` on the original run)

So the two most apples-to-apples runs currently in the PR are:
- `seed=1337`: `1.01558307`
- `seed=1340`: `1.01452726`

The clean 2-seed mean across those matched-environment runs is `1.01505517`.

﻿Added the final clean matched-environment support seed to the record folder:
- `seed=1341`: `final_int8_zlib_roundtrip_exact val_loss:2.28350588 val_bpb:1.01359392`

The clean comparable runs are now:
- `seed=1337`: `1.01558307`
- `seed=1340`: `1.01452726`
- `seed=1341`: `1.01359392`

Clean 3-seed mean across those matched-environment runs:
- `val_bpb:1.01456808`

These three runs are closely matched in throughput / stop depth:
- `1337`: `4108` steps, `145.94ms` step avg
- `1340`: `4094` steps, `146.50ms` step avg
- `1341`: `4093` steps, `146.57ms` step avg

So the clean apples-to-apples reruns now consistently reproduce the original result in the same `~1.014-1.016` band.

Really interesting approach! I was wondering about [line 205](https://github.com/openai/parameter-golf/pull/144/changes#diff-49393f3b459b944faae34a2b572e561f81f7e4c156a5ebdd6befcb97f22a48b7R205) of train_gpt.py. Could this have an effect on val_bpb?

Yep, that definitely could affect val_bpb — thanks for catching it. I confirmed the line in the committed source and I’m retesting with the corrected SentencePiece leading-space marker handling now. I’ll post the corrected logs/results as soon as the rerun finishes.

﻿I confirmed the bug and pushed a correction.

The issue was in `build_sentencepiece_luts`: the SentencePiece leading-space marker check was committed as `piece.startswith("?")` instead of `piece.startswith("?")`, which invalidated the previously reported BPB values.

I've now updated the record folder to:
- fix the marker check in the submitted `train_gpt.py`
- add a corrected rerun log: `mpk_seed_1341_bpbfix.txt`
- update `README.md` and `submission.json` to reflect the corrected numbers

Sadly the corrected rerun result:
- `final_int8_zlib_roundtrip_exact val_loss:2.28232567 val_bpb:1.35172182`

So the earlier `~1.01` BPB claim should be disregarded; it was due to the accounting bug above. Thanks again for catching it.
